what_it_does: Acceptance test for session point-in-time operations. It builds a synthetic JSONL transcript that mimics real compaction shapes, then checks timeline construction, point parsing and cut resolution, materialization, deterministic UUID remapping, launch-shaped verification, registry records, cwd resolution, and an end-to-end supervisor wake/consult flow driven through a stub Claude binary.
observations: The synthetic fixture is load-bearing: line numbers and UUID suffixes are hard-coded and asserted.
It distinguishes a real fork-head boundary from a later payload copy of the same boundary line.
It checks both re-append era and reference era compaction head styles.
Determinism is verified by comparing SHA-256 hashes of two materializations using the same new session id.
It asserts that UUIDs quoted inside prose are not mutated while full-field UUIDs/sessionIds are remapped.
verify_materialized is tested adversarially by appending a stale source sessionId line.
The end-to-end section starts session_supervisor.py on a fixed port, drives it via FsStore mail, and inspects stub argv captures.
A global PASS counter is incremented by a custom check helper.
standouts: The fixture encodes two compaction styles plus a payload boundary copy to exercise deduplication.
Materialization determinism and source byte-integrity are checked with cryptographic hashes.
The supervisor flow is tested against a stub binary rather than a real Claude process.
conventions: A module-level docstring states the tested requirements and references a measured real session shape.
Internal helpers use leading underscore names.
The test uses tempfile and shutil.rmtree for isolation.
Some imports happen inside functions rather than at the top of the file.
Assertions mix a custom check helper with manual raise AssertionError calls.
concerns: Hard-coded line numbers and UUID suffixes make the test brittle to fixture changes.
The end-to-end test binds to a fixed port and uses timing loops, which can be flaky in shared environments.
Late imports reduce static discoverability of dependencies.
The global PASS counter is mutated by the check helper.
Manual AssertionError raises bypass the check helper's accounting.
notes: A dense, fixture-heavy acceptance test that couples synthetic transcript shape to exact line numbers and also runs a live supervisor subprocess.
questions: What is the exact implementation of runtime/session_pointintime and how do uuid5 remapping and point parsing work?
How does encode_project_dir handle edge cases beyond root and dot?
What is the full HTTP and mail contract expected by the supervisor?
How does the supervisor behave when the same point is requested concurrently?
What is the intended behavior when multiple transcript lines share the same timestamp for a ts point?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — R3.3/R3.4 unit and structural guarantees plus the supervisor at-launch flow without a real Claude — matches the implemented tests.
apparent_themes: Fidelity-preserving fork with selective id remapping
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that point-in-time materialization is correct, deterministic, source-preserving, and integrable with the supervisor's resume/spawn flow, while avoiding live Claude invocations.
novelty: It combines a synthetic transcript mirroring measured real-session compaction shapes with an adversarial verifier test and a stub-based end-to-end supervisor flow in a single acceptance script.
contribution: Provides a self-contained acceptance harness that exercises timeline parsing, materialization correctness, deterministic remapping, verification teeth, cwd resolution, and supervisor resume routing using a synthetic fixture and a stub Claude binary.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for session point-in-time materialization and supervisor resume flow using a synthetic compaction transcript and a stub Claude binary.
intention_for_embedding: To validate R3.3 timeline construction and R3.4 materialization behavior plus the supervisor at-launch wake/consult flow without invoking a real Claude.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition, increments a global pass counter, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: Custom assertion helper used throughout the test. It maintains the PASS tally and fails the test with a labeled message when the condition is false.

symbols.does: Constructs a transcript event dictionary with uuid, parent, type, timestamp, and extra fields.
symbols.description: Factory used to build synthetic JSONL events for the fixture, supplying the source session id and allowing arbitrary additional keyword arguments.

symbols.does: Writes a synthetic JSONL transcript with two compactions and a payload boundary copy to a temp file.
symbols.description: Creates the load-bearing fixture used by all checks, encoding a fork-head boundary, a reference-style boundary, preserved runs, metadata lines, attachments, and post-compaction turns.

symbols.does: Computes the SHA-256 hash of a file.
symbols.description: Small utility used to verify source byte integrity and deterministic materialization output by hashing file contents.

symbols.does: Runs the full acceptance suite: unit checks, adversarial verification, and end-to-end supervisor flow.
symbols.description: The top-level test driver that builds fixtures, exercises timeline and materializer functions, checks determinism and registry records, and launches a stub-based supervisor end-to-end test.

symbols.does: Posts a wake or consult mail to the source session via FsStore with a point and copy count.
symbols.description: Nested helper used in the end-to-end section to enqueue supervisor mail and obtain a thread identifier for reply polling.

symbols.does: Polls FsStore for replies matching a given thread until enough arrive or a timeout passes.
symbols.description: Nested helper used in the end-to-end section to collect asynchronous supervisor replies and validate routing and content.
